scope_boundary: Local five-sample repository-task timing and required-output validation only. The probe selects a scoped workflow-trace validator over a broader local baseline and rejects a no-op shortcut; it does not establish stable speedup, production workload quality, model quality, or economic outcomes.
transition_reason: The workload-quality probe is a useful measured route-selection artifact because it requires a passing quality predicate and rejects a cheaper no-op route. The measurement is still local, narrow, and machine-load sensitive, so it blocks promotion for any broader workload-quality, stable-speedup, deployed-scheduler, or economic claim.
required_artifacts: external or live workload-facing quality review
stable repeated timing under controlled conditions
negative_results: cheaper no-op success-text route is rejected
selected route remains scoped to the workflow-trace validator
baseline remains broader than the scoped task
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: selected route no longer satisfies the required output
local timing described as stable speedup or external workload quality
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed workload-quality claim after a public-safe workload suite, controlled repeated measurement, residual and human-repair accounting, external or live review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent workload-quality, scheduler, economics, or benchmarking audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove production workload quality, model quality, scheduler behavior, or economic outcomes
does not create an upward support-state transition
